behaviors.description: Cache keys generated from identical query parameters are always identical, and keys from different parameters never collide.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is key-generation logic (canonicalization + hashing/serialization) inside one local function/object. No real DB/network/OS/LLM dependency is needed. Rejected Integration/System because behavior text only asserts deterministic mapping from query parameters, not cross-module or real-infra effects.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the key generator directly with parameter permutations: same semantic params (including reordered maps/lists) must yield identical keys; distinct params across a large corpus must yield distinct keys.

behaviors.description: The TTL for a cache entry is computed correctly based on the data category's configured freshness rule (e.g., user profiles = 5 minutes, product catalog = 1 hour).
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic rule evaluation (category -> TTL) and optional clock arithmetic within local logic. No real infrastructure or model output is required. Rejected Integration/System because behavior text is about computation correctness from configured rules, not config loading pipeline or runtime environment.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test TTL computation with injected config and fixed clock: assert exact TTL outputs for each category and boundary/default cases (unknown category, zero/negative guardrails if applicable).

behaviors.description: When memory usage exceeds the configured threshold, the eviction strategy switches from LRU to LFU as specified in the configuration.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is at module handoff: memory-pressure signal + config + strategy selector must interact correctly. A pure unit of selector logic can miss wiring/state propagation bugs. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real OS memory telemetry; in-process simulated usage crossing threshold is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test wiring of cache manager, config provider, and strategy selector in-process: drive memory usage metric below/above threshold and assert active strategy transitions LRU->LFU (and any hysteresis rules) exactly per config.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration brake check: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement in this behavior text.

behaviors.description: A write to the underlying PostgreSQL table invalidates all cache entries whose keys match the affected rows.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on real PostgreSQL write/invalidation path. Failures often appear only with real DB semantics (transactions, trigger/event ordering, commit visibility, listener behavior), which mocks can hide. Rejected Integration because in-process fakes cannot faithfully reproduce PostgreSQL invalidation timing/semantics.
behaviors.test_description: System test with real PostgreSQL instance and real cache layer: seed cache for rows, perform actual INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE on target table, wait for invalidation mechanism, then verify matching keys are removed while unrelated keys remain.

behaviors.description: The eviction policy removes the correct entries — least-recently-used under LRU, least-frequently-used under LFU — when the cache is full.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is eviction algorithm correctness within cache data-structure logic (recency/frequency bookkeeping and victim selection). No real OS/network/DB/LLM required. Rejected Integration because behavior text targets policy correctness itself, not cross-module switching or external signals (covered separately by behavior 3).
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test eviction structures directly: construct full cache state, perform accesses to shape recency/frequency, insert one more item, and assert exact victim key under LRU and LFU scenarios (including tie-break rules).
